description_long: This object is a rectangular block constructed from irregularly shaped, rough-hewn stones stacked together, forming a solid and sturdy structure. The stones vary in size and color, with visible gaps and uneven surfaces, giving the block a rustic, natural appearance. The top surface is relatively flat, composed of closely packed stones, while the sides reveal the individual contours and textures of each stone. At the base, some green moss and grass are visible, suggesting outdoor placement and exposure to the elements. The coloration ranges from light gray to tan, with darker patches and weathering marks. The overall impression is of a robust, timeworn stone block, likely used as a barrier or boundary in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A rugged, rectangular block made of stacked, irregular stones with a flat top and weathered, mossy base. The surface is rough and natural, showing signs of outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stacked stones with visible gaps and a mossy base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the flat top and the thin profile of the block, with some vegetation along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the stone arrangement and weathered surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the flat top and the irregular, mossy edge.
attribute_location_context: historic urban retaining wall or garden boundary
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss growth at the base and some surface discoloration indicate exposure to moisture and age. The stones show minor chipping and roughness from weathering.
